summary.overallReadiness: Mostly launch-ready for static browsing, with specific high-risk areas around Real Spawn Data payloads, dynamic API endpoints, and large static assets.
summary.staticClientSideStatus: Most heavy filtering/search behavior is client-side after initial page/data load. /zones is static. /picks and crafting filtering are client-side. Spawn-map interaction is client-side and should not trigger server calls.
summary.primaryRisk: Real Spawn Data detail pages can currently read and serialize very large per-zone loot/detail JSON through the dynamic /zones/[slug] route when source=spawns is active.
summary.recommendedLaunchPosture: Keep the current UI stable, avoid broad rewrites before launch, and prioritize payload splitting/caching for the few systems that can create large Vercel Function or data-transfer spikes.

largestRuntimeDataFiles.risk: Large client import risk when pulled into interactive components.
Adds significant payload to crafting route if bundled or serialized eagerly.
Moderate route payload for spell browsing.
Loaded by /zones Real Spawn mode; acceptable for now but worth watching.
Large detail source; should stay server-side or split by zone.

largestReportOrDebugFiles.risk: Very large report/debug artifact. Keep out of client bundles and runtime imports.
Large per-zone loot/debug file; dangerous if serialized into route payloads.
Large per-zone loot/debug file; dangerous if serialized into route payloads.
Large per-zone loot/debug file; dangerous if serialized into route payloads.
Large per-zone loot/debug file; dangerous if serialized into route payloads.

zoneMapAssets.risk: Good route splitting because map geometry is fetched per zone, but it adds one static asset request and bandwidth per map view.

pageAudits.dataLoading: Imports snapshot summaries and Real Spawn summaries into the client-side explorer.
pageAudits.filtering: Client-side search, expansion, level, race, and sort filtering.
pageAudits.risk: Moderate client bundle/data size from Real Spawn summaries, but it should not create repeated server requests.

pageAudits.dataLoading: Zone Snapshot uses imported summary/detail data. Real Spawn mode reads per-zone summary/spawn/loot JSON from disk and passes data into the client component.
pageAudits.risk: Critical for Real Spawn pages if large loot/debug files are serialized into the response.

pageAudits.rendering: Client-side SVG map interaction with lazy per-zone static JSON fetch.
pageAudits.dataLoading: Map asset manifest is small; full map geometry fetches from public/zone-map-assets only when a map is shown.
pageAudits.filtering: Client-side. Zoom, pan, hover, selection, and Z fading should not call the server.
pageAudits.risk: Bandwidth and DOM/render cost for large maps. Edge request count increases by one static asset per map view.

pageAudits.dataLoading: No per-hover item fetch observed. Authenticated users may load/save preview settings through /api/user/settings.
pageAudits.risk: Mousemove state updates can cause unnecessary rerenders; large item detail imports can increase route payload.

pageAudits.dataLoading: Large recipe datasets and item details are available to the interactive client UI.
pageAudits.risk: Large first-load payload, but low repeated server-request risk.

pageAudits.dataLoading: Pick zone data and compact zone snapshots are loaded locally.
pageAudits.risk: Low server load risk; watch page weight if more summaries are added.

pageAudits.rendering: Mostly static/generated pages and client item previews.
pageAudits.dataLoading: Raid/group data is relatively small compared with crafting/zones.
pageAudits.filtering: Mostly client-side browsing and hover previews.
pageAudits.risk: Low to moderate. Main risk is shared item preview data weight.

edgeRequests: Every static map JSON fetch under public/zone-map-assets adds an Edge Request.
Large PNG hero/background assets can add substantial data transfer under a traffic spike.
Item preview settings, user settings, and auth-related calls can add API requests for signed-in users.
functionInvocations: /zones/[slug] is dynamic and can invoke server logic per request.
/api/status has revalidate=0 behavior and can become expensive if polled frequently.
/api/bonus/reports uses no-store DB-backed behavior and should not be auto-polled under load.
/api/user/settings is useful but can fan out across providers/components for authenticated users.
isrReads: Static API routes with revalidate=86400 are safer than no-store endpoints but still create cache activity when requested.
Mostly static pages should scale well if large dynamic routes are kept under control.
dataTransfer: Real Spawn Data per-zone loot serialization is the largest data-transfer risk.
Map assets total roughly 55 MB across all zones, but are split per route.
Large public PNGs should be compressed before expected traffic increases.

criticalBeforeLaunch.system: Real Spawn Data zone detail pages
criticalBeforeLaunch.evidence: app/zones/[slug]/page.tsx reads per-zone summary, spawn, and loot JSON when source=spawns. Several loot/debug files are tens of MB.
criticalBeforeLaunch.impact: High Function response size, high data transfer, slower TTFB/hydration, and possible memory pressure during traffic spikes.
criticalBeforeLaunch.recommendation: Do not serialize full per-zone loot/debug data by default. Split Real Spawn details into small per-zone summaries plus deferred per-mob/drop chunks, or a cached static JSON endpoint loaded only after expansion. Keep giant debug/report files out of route payloads.

criticalBeforeLaunch.evidence: The status endpoint is configured as no-store/revalidate=0.
criticalBeforeLaunch.impact: If the UI or external monitors poll this frequently, Function Invocations can spike quickly.
criticalBeforeLaunch.recommendation: Add short TTL caching, avoid client polling, or move frequent health checks to a lightweight static/edge-safe response.

criticalBeforeLaunch.evidence: Bonus reports are DB-backed and fetched with cache: no-store.
criticalBeforeLaunch.impact: Admin/report views can produce repeated Function Invocations and database load during launch traffic.
criticalBeforeLaunch.recommendation: Avoid auto-refresh, paginate/cap report queries, add short server-side caching where safe, and keep the route gated.

criticalBeforeLaunch.evidence: Several public PNG assets are 2.3-2.6 MB.
criticalBeforeLaunch.impact: High bandwidth and slower first page loads if these are widely requested.
criticalBeforeLaunch.recommendation: Convert large PNGs to WebP/AVIF, use responsive image sizes, and avoid loading non-critical art above the fold.

recommendedSoon.evidence: 185 per-zone map JSON assets total about 55 MB; 26 are over 500 KB and one exceeds 1 MB.
recommendedSoon.impact: Good route splitting, but map-heavy traffic increases static asset requests and bandwidth.
recommendedSoon.recommendation: Keep map loading deferred to Real Spawn mode, simplify/quantize geometry where possible, load only the active layer first, and consider canvas rendering for very large maps.

recommendedSoon.evidence: ZoneDetailExplorer imports large supporting datasets including item details and map/debug metadata.
recommendedSoon.impact: Large hydration payload and slower client startup on zone detail pages.
recommendedSoon.recommendation: Move item detail lookup and native map debug data behind dynamic imports or per-zone/per-item lazy chunks.

recommendedSoon.evidence: Hover previews are client-side, but mousemove can update preview state frequently.
recommendedSoon.impact: CPU churn on pages with many item rows, especially while users move the mouse across dense drop lists.
recommendedSoon.recommendation: Throttle position updates or update only when cursor movement exceeds a small threshold.

recommendedSoon.evidence: Crafting recipe data plus spell research recipes exceed 7 MB before item details.
recommendedSoon.impact: Large initial route payload for crafting users.
recommendedSoon.recommendation: Split crafting data by tradeskill or load inactive tradeskills lazily after first render.

recommendedSoon.impact: Safe if server-only, risky if accidentally imported into client components.
recommendedSoon.recommendation: Keep detail rows route-scoped or split by zone to reduce accidental client bundling and server memory overhead.

recommendedSoon.evidence: Some maps render thousands of line elements plus spawn dots.
recommendedSoon.impact: Large DOM trees can lag on lower-end devices even without server load.
recommendedSoon.recommendation: Memoize geometry, avoid rerendering static map lines on hover, and evaluate canvas for dense maps.

niceToHaveLater.recommendation: Add bundle/data size budgets for major routes and static JSON assets.
Add lightweight web-vitals and route timing logs around /zones and Real Spawn pages during launch.
Verify Brotli/gzip is active for public JSON files and consider pre-optimized map geometry formats.
Move very large investigation reports out of runtime-adjacent data folders or clearly prevent imports from app code.

niceToHaveLater.system: Service worker or persistent client cache
niceToHaveLater.recommendation: Cache map JSON and large lookup chunks client-side for repeat zone browsing sessions.

quickWins: Compress or replace the largest PNG assets before the traffic spike.
Ensure Real Spawn map geometry only loads when Real Spawn mode is active.
Avoid polling /api/status and /api/bonus/reports from public pages.
Keep map/debug/report files out of client imports.
Add a cap or lazy-load boundary before rendering deep spawn/drop details.

actions: Split or defer Real Spawn loot payloads on /zones/[slug]?source=spawns.
Audit no-store API routes for accidental public polling.
Confirm map assets are fetched only on Real Spawn map views.
Shard item details and crafting data by route/use case.
Move large debug indexes behind lazy imports or remove them from client components.
Add telemetry for route payload size, map load time, and client render time.
Evaluate canvas rendering for dense maps.
Add service-worker caching for static zone-map assets.
Introduce formal data-size budgets in CI.

behaviorChangeStatus.notes: This audit intentionally records risks and recommendations without changing application behavior.
